ddls:
CREATE TABLE "airports" (
  "airport_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each airport */,
  "airport_name" TEXT /* Name of the airport */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the airport is located */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the airport is located */,
  "iata_code" TEXT /* IATA code of the airport */,
  "icao_code" TEXT /* ICAO code of the airport */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the airport */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the airport */,
  "elevation" INTEGER /* Elevation of the airport in feet */,
  "status" TEXT /* Operational status of the airport (e.g., U for open) */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the airport */,
  "airport_type" TEXT /* Type of airport (e.g., commercial, private) */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the airport data (e.g., OurAirports) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the airport record was created */,
  "modified_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the airport record was last modified */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the airport record */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the airport record */,
  "region" INTEGER /* ID of the region where the airport is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("airport_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_airports_country FOREIGN KEY ("country") REFERENCES countries ("country_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_airports_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES airport_statuses ("status_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_airports_region FOREIGN KEY ("region") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_airports_airport_type FOREIGN KEY ("airport_type") REFERENCES airport_types ("type_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_airports_city FOREIGN KEY ("city") REFERENCES cities ("city_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "airport_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each airport type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the airport type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the airport type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "airport_statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each airport status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., U for open) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country where the city is located */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cities_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the airport information */,
  "airport_id" INTEGER /* ID of the airport being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the airport information was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, update) */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the access event (e.g., changes made during an update) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user who accessed the airport information */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_airport_id FOREIGN KEY ("airport_id") REFERENCES airports ("airport_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "airport_services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "airport_id" INTEGER /* ID of the airport offering the service */,
  "service_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the service type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_airport_services_airport_id FOREIGN KEY ("airport_id") REFERENCES airports ("airport_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_airport_services_service_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_type_id") REFERENCES service_types ("service_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_types" (
  "service_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service type */,
  "service_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the service type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_type_id")
)
